target.name: Swarm Aero
target.founder: Danny Goodman, Oliver Palmer, Peter Kalogiannis

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $8M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Swarm Aero is a Seed-stage company in the Defense & Security sector that has raised $8M to date. The broader sector comp universe includes 19 private peers (anchored by Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems) and 2 public comparables (DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS), though a narrow peer set of directly comparable stage and valuation-tier companies proved insufficient for statistical median analysis.

The stage-progression method is the applicable valuation approach here. Using $8M in total capital raised as a proxy for current valuation, a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of progression from Seed to next-stage funding implies a valuation of $20M. Peer-median analysis was not applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the Defense & Security sector; only 0 same-stage peers and 1 adjacent-stage peer met filter criteria, falling short of the minimum 3-point threshold needed for reliable triangulation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $17M (low) to $23M (high), with a median point of $20M. This range reflects stage-typical progression assumptions applied to observed capitalization. Key uncertainties center on the thinness of directly comparable peers at this stage and sector, the degree to which $8M raised reflects true post-money valuation versus price discovery yet to occur, and comparable company trajectory-weighting in a fast-moving defense-tech segment.
